subtasks.description: Query Postgres to retrieve all licenses expiring within the next 6 months, including license ID, product name, expiration date, vendor, and current status
Analyze retrieved licenses and generate renewal recommendations for each, considering expiration urgency, vendor history, and renewal patterns

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks with minimal decomposition: (1) sql_gen at small tier for a straightforward single-table or simple join query filtering licenses by expiration date within 6 months; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to generate recommendations by analyzing the license data (posture/priority classification and recommendation generation are core cross_recon functions, not separate policy_action). No policy_action is needed since renewal recommendations are operational analysis, not governance compliance. Dependency: recommendations depend on successful SQL retrieval.

output.sql: SELECT DISTINCT cf.license_id, cf.product, invf.due_date as expiration_date, invf.vendor_name, invf.status FROM consumption_facts cf JOIN invoice_facts invf ON cf.license_id::text = invf.invoice_id::text WHERE invf.due_date >= CURRENT_DATE AND invf.due_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '6 months' ORDER BY invf.due_date ASC
